fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Hawaii Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Hawaii - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity hawaii, how to transfer dietitian license to hawaii, dietitian compact hawaii, hawaii dietitian endorsement application

board.name: State Licensing Section - Dietitians
board.parent_dept: Hawaii Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Care Assurance
board.mailing_address:
DCCA, PVL Licensing Branch
P. O. Box 3469
Honolulu, HI 96801
board.verify_url: N/A - No state license for dietitians or nutritionists to verify.

quick_facts.ce_requirement: 75 hours every 5 years, must maintain active CDR

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: The credential to practice dietetics in Hawaii, overseen by the Department of Health. The application process is primarily paper-based.

steps.title: No State Licensure Required
steps.description: Hawaii state law does not mandate licensure for individuals practicing as dietitians or nutritionists. There is no state application process through the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs (DCCA) or any other state entity.

steps.title: National Certification is Voluntary
steps.description: While not a state requirement, many professionals pursue national certification, such as the Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN) credential from the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR), for professional recognition and marketability. This is a national, not state, process.

steps.title: Employer and Payer Requirements
steps.description: Prospective employers or insurance providers in Hawaii may still require national certifications (e.g., RDN) or specific academic qualifications to hire dietitians or for reimbursement purposes, even without state licensure.

steps.title: Review Professional Standards
steps.description: Even without state licensure, professionals are encouraged to adhere to ethical guidelines and standards of practice established by national professional organizations like the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics.

documents.detail: Obtained by calling the DOH and completed accurately.
A copy of your active Commission on Dietetic Registration card.
Proof of the required accredited degree (foreign degrees require validation).

provisional_text: Hawaii does not explicitly offer a temporary or provisional license according to current state licensing regulations. Passing the CDR exam is required prior to applying for a license.
reciprocity_text: Hawaii allows for licensure by endorsement. Individuals holding a valid dietitian certification or license from another state, territory, or jurisdiction may be eligible if the requirements are equal to or greater than Hawaii's (maintaining an active CDR typically qualifies).

faqs.question: How much does a Hawaii dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial non-refundable fee for a Hawaii Dietitian License is $225.

faqs.question: When does the Hawaii license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Hawaii dietitian licenses are valid for three years from the date of issue and must be renewed triennially. You should contact the DOH to confirm current renewal fees.

faqs.question: Can I apply for a Hawaii dietitian license online?
faqs.answer: No. You must contact the State Licensing Section directly at (808) 692-7400 to request the application packet.

faqs.question: Does Hawaii have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Hawaii. To work in Hawaii, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Hawaii?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Hawaii while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.processing_time: N/A - No state license is required.
reciprocity.endorsement_url: N/A - No state licensure or endorsement process.
reciprocity.notes: Hawaii allows for licensure by endorsement. Individuals holding a valid dietitian certification or license from another state, territory, or jurisdiction may be eligible if the requirements are equal to or greater than Hawaii's (maintaining an active CDR typically qualifies).

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Hawaii
hero.dek: Hawaii does not require state-level licensure for dietitians or nutritionists. Therefore, there is no state application portal or board to apply through for this profession.
